channel.title: Comments for page "About the Serpent"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "About the Serpent"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, and I'm thoroughly impressed with the amount of research involved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took me about an hour and a half to translate, and three times that to read up on the assorted Chinese lore and compile it into footnotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This was amazing! The style and prose are fantastic and I loved the interpretations of the various groups and SCPs. The art was also really nice and added to the overall feel of the article.

One of my new favorite tales, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a shame that you cannot add in one of the origins of the Liu last name. That they were descendents of Dragon keepers/slayers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really appreciate the Xia for putting a lot of Foundation stuff through a very different cultural lens to what we're used to. And I love it for the epic high Chinese fantasy/myth feel. This tale continues the trend by nailing both those things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Xia Dynasty stuff has always been interesting to me, as it takes a fair amount of the preexisting Sarkic and CotBG stuff and places it in a setting that is usually not used that often outside the Xia skips and tales, to my knowledge. Each segment here reads as a nice tiny bite of mythology, doing a good job of worldbuilding without overstaying its welcome. On top of that, the art included before each section really does add a nice touch of flavor.

I really liked reading this as a draft, and I like it now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very well done! I am passingly familiar with most of the Chinese elements, and the way you linked them to the Foundationverse is most enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how your tales take various aspects of the SCPverse and merge them in something great. As Roget pointed, there's a lot potential here, I can see this becoming a Chinese mythology equivalent of ETDP, only that instead of action there's good scented prose!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks a lot Roget :3

Although did it really? Cause I mean, I added Bumo in, but I didn't really alter the general plot of that story XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed the fable style stories here. I still feel like there's a lot of untapped potential to make something good into something great but what's here isn't bad. In fact, it's very good. I especially enjoyed About The Beasts for going against my expectations. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, and it adds onto the depth that the other articles on the anomalous Xia culture started.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically, I saw this awesome tale, The Parables of Pseudo-Nadox, and think, hey, I should write some parables of SCPverse too! But instead I'm gonna write like lengthy Chinese text first and then translate them, because that will surely make it look authentic :D (Also that I can recycle all these old snippets I wrote.)

Great thanks to Lex1nat0r, Metaphysician, Conwell, Roget, Dora, Overandover, SpectralDragon, MrWrong, minmin, BitVyper, TheRaven, ZacharyMaxwell, Dr_Leonard, djkaktus, Vacuum, and all others who read the tale.

Art by me :3

+ List of SCP & Myth/Legend references
- List of SCP & Myth/Legend references

The legend of Liu Bang slaying a white serpent: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emperor_Gaozu_of_Han#Insurrection_against_the_Qin_dynasty

About the Gods

  • Fuxi/Father Serpent/伏羲 - Mekhane
  • Nüwa/Mother Dragon/女娲 - Yaldabaoth
  • Qilin/麒麟 - SCP-2845 - THE DEER
  • Pangu/盘古 - Chinese creation god: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pangu ("天地始如鸡子" based on "天地混沌如鸡子" from 《三王历纪》)
  • Guixu/归墟 - A bottomless pit in Chinese myth where all water eventually end up to
  • Nine Children/九子 - In Chinese myth, the dragon spawns nine different beasts: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nine_sons_of_the_dragon
  • Taisui/太岁 - A invented planet opposing Jupiter: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tai_Sui

About the Kingdoms

  • Kingdom of Apes/猿之国 - SCP-1000 - Bigfoot
  • Xia Dynasty/夏 - SCP-2481 - Kill the Suns

About the Kings

  • King of Ajia/阿伽之王 - SCP-701 - The Hanged King's Tragedy
  • Three Lords of Death/三阎罗 - Brothers Death (SCP-1440 - The Old Man from Nowhere & Death and the Doctors Hub)
  • Yulü/郁垒 - Chinese god in charge of capturing ghosts/souls
  • Yama/阎摩: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yama
  • Kshitigarbha/地藏: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kshitigarbha

About the Beasts

  • Yang/殃君 - Grand Karcist Ion (Sarkicism Hub)
  • Daevas/狄瓦 - SCP-140 - An Incomplete Chronicle
  • Bumo/不末 - Bumaro (Church of the Broken God Hub)
  • Yaluo/压罗 - SCP-2095 - The Siege of Gyaros

About Men

  • Panjia/盘甲 - A very much headcanon version of Pangloss from a tale I never get to properly revise and post ._.
  • Mechanical soilders/机巧兵俑 & a flying ship/飞舟 - SCP-580 - Shi Huang Ti's Five Heaven Chariot
  • "He then sank the palace into the ground, and left the emperor with no escape despite his eternal life." - SCP-2101 - The Imperial Army
  • Jianmu/建木 - A tree from the Chinese myth that serves as a bridge between heaven and earth
  • Epang Palace/阿房宫: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epang_Palace
  • Shu Huang Ti's burning of books: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Burning_of_books_and_burying_of_scholars
  • Xu Fu/徐福: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xu_Fu

The things Panjia mentioned about the ancient times are actually: Tower of Babel, Eden, and Kunlun: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kunlun_Mountains#Mythology

Yufu/鱼凫 & Cancong/蚕丛 are both mythical kings of Shu state, but here I'm actually adapting them the whole Xia series, and have little to do with the actual myth ._.
